claim: Small AI—local, efficient, open-source models running directly on devices—enables economically viable agentic workflows at scale, reducing reasoning costs from $25+ per chain to near-zero
actionable: For multi-step agentic reasoning pipelines, prototype with small local models first to validate economics before scaling with cloud models, as local inference can be 100x faster
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: ARM-based devices (laptops, Chromebooks, MacBooks, Windows on ARM) and edge hardware can run 20B-parameter models with sub-second latency locally without cloud connectivity
actionable: Target ARM-based devices for local model deployment and test with Gemma 3, Qwen 3, or Llama models in the 7B-13B range that fit on standard consumer hardware
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Domain-specific fine-tuning of small models (e.g., financial regulation understanding in 7B model) outperforms using frontier models for specialized tasks while being deployable locally
actionable: For vertical domains (fintech, healthcare, legal), fine-tune a small open model on domain-specific documents and regulatory data instead of using large frontier models
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Agentic workflows with local small models avoid prohibitive reasoning costs that plague 90% of failing AI projects; moving costs toward zero unlocks cost-effective prototyping
actionable: Build agentic systems with local small models for iterative development and cost-effective experimentation instead of betting the company on frontier model reasoning chains
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Raspberry Pi 5 can run multi-model agentic stacks including Gemma, Qwen, embeddings, speech-to-text, and speech-to-speech with autonomous reasoning and learning capabilities
actionable: Use Raspberry Pi as a test platform for local agentic workflows with open-source models; if your use case works on RPi5, it'll work on all consumer hardware
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Stable Audio small model runs 30x faster on ARM devices with Scalable Matrix Extensions (SME), showing hardware acceleration dramatically improves small AI performance
actionable: Prioritize ARM-based hardware with SME/NPU acceleration for audio/media workloads; expect 20-30x performance gains as new hardware with SME arrives in laptops and phones
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Vertical expertise combined with small model fine-tuning creates sustainable competitive advantage; DevOps/MLOps skills applied to small AI deployment become scarce and defensible
actionable: Build your competitive moat by combining domain expertise (fintech, healthcare, legal) with small model deployment skills (quantization, optimization, fine-tuning) rather than competing on model size
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Microcontrollers can run tiny ML models for storytelling, HMI authentication, and object detection, expanding AI to embedded/IoT devices beyond traditional servers
actionable: Explore TinyML use cases for embedded devices: toys, appliances, smart home systems; models under 1M parameters can provide useful functionality on microcontrollers
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing

claim: Local small AI deployment ensures data privacy and security since computation happens on-device with no cloud data transmission or exposure to API leaks
actionable: For sensitive applications (healthcare, legal, financial), deploy local small models to eliminate cloud data transmission risks and ensure data stays on-device
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Eric Sondhi: Small AI: The Next Big Thing
